Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "fat_title_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "funkis_cloud_title_id" ; FAT Title::Funkis Cloud Title id ) & SetProperty ( "title_environment" ; FAT Title::Title Environment )
FAT Title::Title isLive Or FAT Title::Title Backend ≠ "Funkis Cloud"
Not FAT Title::Title isLive Or FAT Title::Title Backend ≠ "Funkis Cloud"

StyledText.Data: <- Containers used for import and export

Calculation.Text: "View all media with subtitles in title"
"View all text styles defined in title"
"Choose a unique title that will be used in JSON references"

StyledText.Data: The reference title is the name of the title as it is seen by the users of Funkis Authoring Tool, Funkis Cloud and Funkis Insights. The name is not displayed to the end-user. The reference title is also the base for the friendly-id. For Base projects it is only used for reference in FAT. The reference title will automatically be prettified when needed.
Add a Package to this Title
This is the file suffix all original text-json files will be exported with.
Decide what kind of format the delivery of this Title will be in.

Calculation.Text: "Select the delivery format for this Title. LMS Only"

StyledText.Data: The backend determines which functionality will be available on the server and affects mainly the use of IOs. Please note, Funkis Authoring Tool will not hide features that cannot be used on Web-server, LMS or xAPI backend. LMS IO data will be saved in suspend_data according to SCORM-standard. IO data cannot be shared between packages. xAPI In development. Funkis Cloud All IO features can be used. All users become members in a group and in a session where all input data is stored and can be shared between packages within the same Title.

The unique identifier of a Title given by Funkis Cloud. The id is generated when creating a new Title. Example "6416f270fc17be0f06b825a5" If changing Title environment - the Title id needs to correspond with the title id on the new environment.

Calculation.Text: "Enter a description of the Title. It's only for authoring and development purposes."

StyledText.Data: Each full path to a package should follow the pattern: “Client directory/Title directory/Package directory/”. However, some older Titles does not follow this pattern.

Calculation.Text: "Enter the directory name for this client. Leave blank for no client directory."

StyledText.Data: This is the name of the clients’ directory where the FAT content will be exported. Each full path to a package should follow the pattern: “Client directory/Title directory/Package directory/”. However, some older Titles does not follow this pattern.

Calculation.Text: "Select a a server environment for this title"
"Select a a server environment for this title"

StyledText.Data: The title environment is for the servers for preview and publish. For Funkis Coud titles they are used for hosting the title for users and groups. Your selected environment: <<Title Environment::Name>> <<Title Environment::Description>>
The environments preview server Your selected preview server: <<Title Server Preview::Name>> <<Title Server Preview::Description>> URL: <<Title Server Preview::URL>>
This is the URL for the LRS endpoint during development of this Title. LRS Key and Secret are both set on Package level.

Calculation.Text: "Enter URL to LRS endpoint for enabling xAPI data sharing features"
"Publish server not available in selected environment"

StyledText.Data: The environments publish server Your selected publish server: <<Title Server Publish::Name>> <<Title Server Publish::Description>> URL: <<Title Server Publish::URL>>

Set this to "1" = TRUE if the title is delivered to end-users. This is the definition of LIVE! Set to "0" = FALSE if Title is only used by developers and reviewers during development. This setting will not affect anything! It is only for documentation purposes.

Calculation.Text: "Check building block compatibility and completion status."

LayoutObject.name: Package icon 2

Calculation.Text: FAT Package::Proxy type = "Chapter" Or FAT Package::Proxy type = "Page"
notDeveloper Or FAT Package::Proxy type = "Chapter" Or FAT Package::Proxy type = "Page"

SetProperty ( "level" ; 1 ) & SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Title" ) & SetProperty ( "server_mode" ; "Preview" ) & SetProperty ( "package_dir" ; FAT Package::Export Package Sub Path ) & SetProperty ( "package_friendly_id" ; FAT Package::JSON Friendly id ) & SetProperty ( "package_type_name" ; FAT Package::Package type name ) & SetProperty ( "fat_version" ; FAT Package::FAT version number )
IsEmpty(FAT Title::FC Title directory) /* Denna skall ändras när Package-flytten är klar - skall referera till reference title också */ Or FAT Package::Proxy type ≠ ""

SetProperty ( "level" ; 1 ) & SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Title" ) & SetProperty ( "server_mode" ; "Preview" ) & SetProperty ( "package_dir" ; FAT Package::Export Package Sub Path ) & SetProperty ( "package_friendly_id" ; FAT Package::JSON Friendly id ) & SetProperty ( "package_type_name" ; FAT Package::Package type name ) & SetProperty ( "fat_version" ; FAT Package::FAT version number )
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )

StyledText.Data: Package directory on server: <<FAT Package::FC Package directory>>

Calculation.Text: PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )

StyledText.Data: <<ƒ:Let ( [ name = Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Name; sub_name = Case ( Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Codebase type = "version"; Baseproject Codebase Version @ Package::Version string; Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Codebase type = "branch"; Baseproject Codebase Version @ Package::Branch string; "" ) ]; name & " " &sub_name )>>

Title publish path: <<<Field Missing>>> Current server that FAT publish to: <<Title Server::Name>> <<Title Server::Description>> The publish path on a server should normally be: “client-directory-name/title-directory-name/package-directory-name/” I.e “woodfirst/trastad/self-study/”. Leave a field blank to skip that level.

CharacterStyle.Text: The publish path on a server should normally be: “client-directory-name/title-directory-name/package-directory-name/” I.e “woodfirst/trastad/self-study/”. Leave a field blank to skip that level.

Calculation.Text: "Deliver all packages to Funkis Cloud production server"
FAT Title::Title Backend ≠ "Funkis Cloud" Or IsEmpty(Title Environment::Publish Server id)

StyledText.Data: Deliver all packages on Preview to Publish

Calculation.Text: FAT Title::Title Backend ≠ "Funkis Cloud" Or IsEmpty(Title Environment::Publish Server id)
"Export all content in all packages to Preview"

StyledText.Data: Import texts from other Funkis tools
Legacy. Import texts from Funkis Designer Tool. Don’t use this function for importing subtitles or SprigStory.

For some Learning Programmes, some texts may have been created outside the Funkis CMT, i.e. from SprigStory or Funkis Designer Tool. These external texts needs to be imported to Funkis CMT to keep all texts in a learning programme intact. In the box below, you’ll find the necessary functions to import and update these external texts, before sending them to translation. All imported texts will be referenced with their source file, which will be used when updating and deleting these texts only. For subtitle text documents, please see
Import subtitles created by Funkis Designer Tool

Funkis CMT can import and update subtitles for multiple media files from a Funkis Designer generated subtitle JSON file. Import subtitles New subtitles will be created in the CMT when importing. A warning will be triggered if importing subtitles that already exist in the CMT. Please note, Subtitle texts that cannot be matched with an exisitng media file in the CMT will NOT be imported! Update subtitles Existing subtitles will be updated with new text and enter and exit times from the selected Funkis Designer subtitle file. New subtitles will be created in CMT for media files that havent’t been subtitled previously. Media with subtitles - Deleting subtitles needs to be done on a media by media basis. - Importing VTT subtitles needs to be done on a media by media basis.

There are several options for file export from Funkis CMT: Local Files are exported to a local folder on the user’s hard drive. This option is only available for developers. Remote Files are sent to the choosen remote server, using the Title id as an identiifier. When exporting remote please allow time for file transfer when refreshing browser. JSON All JSON files - cmt_page.json, cmt_page-item.json, cmt_tag.json and cmt_symbol.json are exported to the JSON folder. Text All text files, including subtitles and imprted texts are exported - in all languages. Updated media Only media files that have been changed since last publish will be exported. This is the only available option for authors and editors. All media All media files are exported. Local cleanup All content in the local destination folder is deleted.

CharacterStyle.Text: Local Files are exported to a local folder on the user’s hard drive. This option is only available for developers. Remote Files are sent to the choosen remote server, using the Title id as an identiifier. When exporting remote please allow time for file transfer when refreshing browser. JSON All JSON files - cmt_page.json, cmt_page-item.json, cmt_tag.json and cmt_symbol.json are exported to the JSON folder. Text All text files, including subtitles and imprted texts are exported - in all languages. Updated media Only media files that have been changed since last publish will be exported. This is the only available option for authors and editors. All media All media files are exported. Local cleanup All content in the local destination folder is deleted.

StyledText.Data: Publish to <<Delivery format>> using project <<GitLab URL>>

Calculation.Text: FAT Title::Delivery format = "" Or FAT Title::GitLab URL = ""
FAT Title::Delivery format = "" Or FAT Title::GitLab URL = ""
"The GitLab URL is given by Baseproject."

StyledText.Data: Tags (or labels) can be used for categorising specific content. Each Title has it’s own unique set of tags. Tags can be used for many different purposes, such as job roles (”technician”, “manager”, etc) or difficulty level (”Easy”, “Medium”, “Hard”). The functionality for the tag is defined by the program code. Tags can be set on Package-level, Chapter-level, Page-level or Page item-level. Check the boxes where tags applies for this Title.
Select the theme identifier that you want to use. This refers to the CSS-class file compiled in the template project on GitLab.
The side menu icon is an optional feature. Leave blank fpr the default icon (hamburger menu).

For productions using native video only. This style will affect each VTT-file exported from CMT adding this style object in the beginning. It seems there's no property that can change the black rectangle behind the text.

For productions using native video only. The cue style will affect each VTT-file entry. The purpose is to get a better look on different browsers. There are five cue settings: vertical Indicates that the text will be displayed vertically rather than horizontally, such as in some Asian languages. vertical:rl writing direction is right to left vertical:lr writing direction is left to right line Specifies where text appears vertically. If vertical is set, line specifies where text appears horizontally. Value can be a line number. The line height is the height of the first line of the cue as it appears on the video. Positive numbers indicate top down. (Integer %-optional) Negative numbers indicate bottom up. position Specifies where the text will appear horizontally. If vertical is set, position specifies where the text will appear vertically.Value is a percentage. Must be an integer (no decimals) between 0 and 100 inclusive. Must be followed by a percent sign (%). size Specifies the width of the text area. If vertical is set, size specifies the height of the text area. Value is a percentage. align Specifies the alignment of the text. Text is aligned within the space given by the size cue setting if it is set. align:start = align left align:center = align centred horizontally align:end = align right
Insert a "fonts.css" file that will be exported to the content/theme/ folder. Only for Site packages (Jan 2022).
Styles override will be added to the "styles" object in them.json exorted to content/theme/ folder. Only for Site packages (Jan 2022).

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "theme_template_id" ; Theme Template::gTheme ) & SetProperty ( "language_code" ; FAT Title::Original language code ) & SetProperty ( "country_code" ; FAT Title::Original country code ) & SetProperty ( "theme_id" ; FAT Theme::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "re_import" ; True )

StyledText.Data: Information needed here - Nina / Jesper - kolla!
If you want to use a unique divider between blocks in the scroll. Insert an image here. The block divider color will be overrided.

Calculation.Text: FAT Theme::Block Divider Scroll ≠ "image" Or IsEmpty ( Media BDI::Media container)

StyledText.Data: The slide logo can max be Width = 315 px and Height 217 px. A good SVG is recommended but a PNG might be the only choice. The logotype can include transparent parts.

LayoutObject.name: Predifened color name

StyledText.Data: Predefined color name HexColor. Client color name
Theme colors. Pre-defined color name is referred via code and cannot be changed.Client color name can be any name.
<<FAT Theme TextStyle::Predefined Text style Name>> Style type: <<FAT Theme TextStyle::Type>>

LayoutObject.name: TextStyle name Copy5
TextStyle name Copy3
TextStyle name Copy4
TextStyle name Copy2
TextStyle FontFamily
TextStyle name Copy6

StyledText.Data: Theme textstyles. Can be chosen in page item, page, chapter and package settings from Funkis Player version 1.13 and forward.
Font size Font weight. Line height Letter space Color Alignment

LayoutObject.name: Predifened color name Copy

StyledText.Data: Add font files that will be exported to the content/theme/ folder. Mark styles included in each font file. Only for Site packages (Jan 2022).

The button radius decides the visual appearance of the navigation buttons in both slide and scroll. (Next, Previous, Menu etc) None = squared button corners. The color of the buttons are set by the colors button_background and button_foreground.

Calculation.Text: Baseproject Codebase @ Title::Short name = "Locked by developer" Or (PatternCount (Baseproject Codebase @ Title::Short name; "project") And IsEmpty(FAT Package::GitLab Project code URL)) //Locked by developer

StyledText.Data: Select a Funkis player version for all packages in this title. You have now selected the Funkis Player version: <<Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Short name>> <<Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Description>> If you change version, make sure to press the Update button to update all packages in the title. This can take several minutes and you will be notified via email when the project on the remote host is updated. Advanced: If you need to use different Funkis Player versions for each package, go to Package settings.

Calculation.Text: "Select Funkis Player for all packages in the title."

LayoutObject.name: Package icon 3

Calculation.Text: FAT Package::Proxy type = "Chapter" Or FAT Package::Proxy type = "Page"
notDeveloper Or FAT Package::Proxy type = "Chapter" Or FAT Package::Proxy type = "Page"
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )
PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )

StyledText.Data: Package directory on server: <<FAT Package::FC Package directory>>

Calculation.Text: PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )

StyledText.Data: <<ƒ:Let ( [ name = Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Name; sub_name = Case ( Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Codebase type = "version"; Baseproject Codebase Version @ Package::Version string; Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Codebase type = "branch"; Baseproject Codebase Version @ Package::Branch string; "" ) ]; name & " " &sub_name )>>
<<ƒ:Let ( [ FATV = Max(Baseproject Codebase @ Title::FAT Version number; Baseproject Codebase Version @ Title::FAT Version number) ]; "Funkis Player uses FAT version " & FATV )>>

Building blocks are graphical elements that contain images, texts or videos that are different for each instance. Funkis CMT fills the blocks with content. Each learning programme has its own set of building blocks, which are declared in this list. The blocks are declared by defining number of text fields, number of media containers and number of content arrays. The column labelled “T” to the left indicates whether the block is based on a template. WARNING! Don’t edit the building blocks in an ongoing production without talking to the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

CharacterStyle.Text: WARNING! Don’t edit the building blocks in an ongoing production without talking to the developers, since they are referenced in the code.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "blocktemplate_id" ;FAT Title::gBlockTemplate ) & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "language_code" ; FAT Title::Original language code ) & SetProperty ( "country_code" ; FAT Title::Original country code )

StyledText.Data: Import a building block from a block template. Available block templates depends on program type - scroll or slide.
Number of building blocks: <<Number of blocks>>

Calculation.Text: FAT Block::Number of page item instances > 0
"View all page items based on this block"
FAT Block::Number of page item instances = 0

StyledText.Data: Block may not work with latest codebase.
Which base project to be used. Also called template project. Leave blank for no base project.

Calculation.Text: "Select which base project to use. Leave blank for no base project"

StyledText.Data: You can choose any name for your attribute. However, the name will be “prettified” using _underscore_in the JSON key.

Choose attribute type depending on how you want to the JSON key value to be formatted. Prettified string Will replace capital letters with lower-case, åäö with aao and spaces with dash “-”. Number An integer value. If empty it will be replaced with 0 (zero). URL encoded string A string where all characters except A-Z, a-z are replaced with %. Media Will enable a media container as an attribute input. The value will be the prettified file name of the media file in the container. The media itself will be included in the media export. String Unaltered key value. Depending on characters, this might create a corrupt JSON. Color A string value like “#345678” or “black” will be accepted. Text A text value will be included in the text export. The text will be localised. The attribute value will NOT include the text in the export, only the text id.

CharacterStyle.Text: A string value like “#345678” or “black” will be accepted.
A text value will be included in the text export. The text will be localised. The attribute value will NOT include the text in the export, only the text id.

StyledText.Data: Decide on which level you want the attribute to be present: Learning programme, Course, Chapter or Page. You cannot create new Attributes for Blocks in this interface. Open or create a Block to add Attributes for the Block. If you want the same attibute to be present at more than one level you need to create one attribute for each level. NOTE! Since 2020-05-28 its no longer possible to create new attributes on Page item level. Now, attributes are added to specific Blocks instead.

CharacterStyle.Text: You cannot create new Attributes for Blocks in this interface. Open or create a Block to add Attributes for the Block.

StyledText.Data: The default value is the initial value for this attribute. Leave blank for no default value. Example “#345678”

Modifier is a possibility to add a new key-value pair to any item in a Title. Key-values can be of many different types. You cannot create new Modifiers for specific Blocks in this interface. Open or create a Block to add Modifier for the Block. If you add or remove a modifier in an ongoing production, all items will add or remove that modifier. The column labelled “T” to the left indicates whether it is based on a template. WARNING! Don’t change the modifiers in an ongoing production if you're not a developer and know what you're doing.

CharacterStyle.Text: You cannot create new Modifiers for specific Blocks in this interface. Open or create a Block to add Modifier for the Block.
The column labelled “T” to the left indicates whether it is based on a template.
WARNING! Don’t change the modifiers in an ongoing production if you're not a developer and know what you're doing.

ParagraphStyle.Text: If you add or remove a modifier in an ongoing production, all items will add or remove that modifier.

StyledText.Data: Decide on which package type you want the attribute to be present: Scroll, Slide or Site. You cannot create new Attributes for Blocks in this interface. Open or create a Block to add Attributes for the Block. If you want the same attibute to be present for more than one package type you need to create one attribute for each package type.

CharacterStyle.Text: You cannot create new Attributes for Blocks in this interface. Open or create a Block to add Attributes for the Block.

StyledText.Data: The attribute description will be visible for authors and editors when adding content and entering key values to the CMT.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "learningprogramme_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey) & SetProperty ( "level" ; FAT Title::gModifierLevel) & SetProperty ( "reset_global_fields" ; True)
"Type a comma-separated list of all available states"

StyledText.Data: Type a comma-separated list with the values you want the user to select from. Example “blue,red,green,gray”
Note: Languages in translation stage lower than 4 will not be included in export .

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty(FAT Localisation::Package id) And Not FAT Localisation::Shared text localisation
SetProperty ( "localisation_id" ; FAT Localisation::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "translation_method" ; FAT Localisation::Translation method) & SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Title")

StyledText.Data: Select a language to add to the translation list. Select a method for translation. The method will decide the following steps.

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty(FAT Title::gSelectedLocalisation) Or IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gTranslationMethod )

StyledText.Data: This is the file suffix all original text-json files will be exported with.
Number of localisations: <<Number of Title Localisations>>
Text in Title was arranged in contextual order: <<TextInContextualOrderTimeStamp>>.

Calculation.Text: "Export all texts in original localisation code to one single JSON file."
FAT Package @ Active Package Group::Package Backend ≠ "lms"
FAT Title::gSelectedPackageGroup = "" Or Not PatternCount (List(FAT Package Group::PrimaryKey) ; FAT Title::gSelectedPackageGroup )

StyledText.Data: Package Groups If you have many Packages - Create Package Groups and assign each Package a group to make authoring easier.

LayoutObject.name: Package Group portal

Calculation.Text: "Remove IO Group - this will NOT remove any IOs"

SetProperty ( "clone_level" ; "Package group" ) & SetProperty ( "item_id" ; FAT Package Group::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "item_parent_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "item_description" ; FAT Package Group::Name )
"Deliver all packages to Funkis Cloud production server"
FAT Title::Title Backend ≠ "Funkis Cloud" Or IsEmpty(Title Environment::Publish Server id) Or notJens
"Duplicate and Move all packages in the group"

StyledText.Data: Select package group for packages From the drop-down menu - select a Package group for each package.

In rare occassions, the JSONs from FAT can be corrupt and Funkis Player will crash on startup. Press validate JSONs to check that all JSONs for Packages, Chapters, Pages and Page items are proper. If a corrupt JSON is found you will be noticed.
View a change log for this Title

When a Title is delivered to the client it’s a good practice to do a project cleanup. Especially when the Title is going to be localised its important to do a cleanup before sending texts to translation.
The cleanup removes all unused Blocks and texts belonging them. Since our clients pay translator agencies by number of words this is important to redcuce costs.
The Optimise database button deletes unused text fields, the text fields that are not needed for the page item according to the block definition. This will optimize database.

Make a full copy Creates a complete clone of everything in the Title. Global texts, modifiers, blocks and settings including content, such as tags, colors, lists, IOs, packages, chapters, pages, page items, texts, media files and page templates. There will be no existing links between the old and the new Title.
Import all platform specific text variables that are available for this title.
In some occasions when content has been moved between different packages, the text fields will not be exported properly. This will fix this problem.

LayoutObject.name: String Number Value

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ Title::Type = "Media" Or FAT Modifier @ Title::Type = "Color" Or FAT Modifier @ Title::Type = "Text" Or FAT Modifier @ Title::Type = "Link"

LayoutObject.name: Color FM
TextStyle Editor
Text TRANS

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ Title::Type ≠ "Text" Or FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode = FAT Title::Original localisation code Or IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode)

LayoutObject.name: Text

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ Title::Type ≠ "Text" Or Not IsEmpty ( FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode) And FAT Title::gCurrentLocalisationCode≠FAT Title::Original localisation code
FAT Modifier @ Title::Type ≠ "Media" Or Not SubtitleMedia ( Media MV TT::Container file extension )

LayoutObject.name: Edit media
Link

StyledText.Data: Users with access to this Title

Basically all text entries in Funkis CMT can be limited in number of characters. The reason is to ensure a good-looking end result. Check with the art director or the developer of the learning programme to learn the number of max characters for titels and subtitles for the navigation-items Learning programem, Course, Chater and Page. When a user is exceeding the amount of characters, a red warning tag will be visible. It is possible to enter more characters than defined here. This function is especially valuable for localisations.

Calculation.Text: PatternCount ( FAT Export Log @ Package::Export Mode Explained ; "code" )

StyledText.Data: Select which Funkis Cloud Title that FAT shall publish to. If this popup menu is empty - please create the Title in both Funkis Cloud and Funkis Insight.
Select which Funkis Cloud Title that FAT shall publish to. If this popup menu is empty - please create the Title in both Funkis Cloud and Funkis Insight.
If True (default) the red Inspector tag to the left of the screen will be visible on all published apps in the program. This will affect all packages and all FAT users.
Decide what kind of format the delivery of this Title will be in.

Calculation.Text: "Arrange text for export in contextual order."
SetProperty ( "publish_mode" ; "CODE_UPDATE" ) & SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Title") & SetProperty ( "show_dialog" ; "true") & SetProperty ( "package_id" ; FAT Package::PrimaryKey)

Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Short name = "Locked by developer" Or (PatternCount (Baseproject Codebase @ Package::Short name; "project") And IsEmpty(FAT Package::GitLab Project code URL)) //Locked by developer Or Package type::Name = "Title info"
